ird_board_count_note: ird NUM BOARDS (e.g. 4× n300 on WH lb) may differ from runtime device_count (e.g. 8 chips). Always use device_count for TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES and mesh.
single_chip_skip_reason: single-chip bringup (n150/p150) requires a dedicated single-chip host (runtime device_count=1). This session has device_count=8 (TT_XLA_ARCH='n300-llmbox'). Fabric/multichip boxes (n300-llmbox, qb, galaxy, WH/BH lb with n300 modules) cannot run single-chip bringup even with TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 — change to a 1-board machine (e.g. nebula_x1(1|1) or p150(1|1)) and re-run /model-bringup.

policy.single_chip: Single-chip bringup (n150/p150) runs only on dedicated single-chip hosts (runtime device_count must be 1). Do not run on n300-llmbox, qb, galaxy, or lb/n300 fabric hosts — pinning TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 does not make bringup valid.
policy.multichip: Multichip bringup uses runtime device_count (chips), not ird board count. Confirm count via probe before TP runs; set TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,...,N-1.

skipped_archs.reason: single-chip bringup (n150/p150) requires a dedicated single-chip host (runtime device_count=1). This session has device_count=8 (TT_XLA_ARCH='n300-llmbox'). Fabric/multichip boxes (n300-llmbox, qb, galaxy, WH/BH lb with n300 modules) cannot run single-chip bringup even with TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 — change to a 1-board machine (e.g. nebula_x1(1|1) or p150(1|1)) and re-run /model-bringup.

single-chip bringup (n150/p150) requires a dedicated single-chip host (runtime device_count=1). This session has device_count=8 (TT_XLA_ARCH='n300-llmbox'). Fabric/multichip boxes (n300-llmbox, qb, galaxy, WH/BH lb with n300 modules) cannot run single-chip bringup even with TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 — change to a 1-board machine (e.g. nebula_x1(1|1) or p150(1|1)) and re-run /model-bringup.

board_vs_chip_correction: device_count=8 CHIPS spread over 4 BOARDS; TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES enumerates boards 0-3 (NOT chips 0-7). Setting 0..7 errors: 'Valid integer IDs are between 0 and 3'.
